title: Gauge-covariant projected entangled paired states for interacting systems in a magnetic field
authors: Wei Tang, Gunnar Möller, Frank Verstraete, Laurens Vanderstraeten
abstract: The Hamiltonian for a system of itinerant particles on a two-dimensional lattice in a uniform magnetic field reduces the translational symmetry to a magnetic translation group, because of the need to choose a particular gauge for the vector potential. Nonetheless, in many situations all physical observables of the ground state remain entirely translation invariant. In this work, we introduce a projected entangled-pair state (PEPS) wavefunction with a pattern of virtual flux tensors, for which all physical expectation values are translation invariant by construction, possibly within an enlarged unit cell reflecting any symmetry breaking in the target state. Moreover, we show that the usual contraction and optimization methods for translation-invariant PEPS can be used, with the magnetic flux per plaquette only entering as a continuous parameter in the tensor network contractions. Therefore, our approach provides a method for simulating an interacting many-body system in a uniform magnetic field independently of the gauge choice for the vector potential and bypassing the need to consider extended magnetic unit cells.
summary: This paper introduces a new tensor network method (PEPS) designed to simulate many-body systems in magnetic fields. By implementing magnetic flux at the virtual level of the tensors, the authors create an ansatz that is gauge-covariant and preserves translation symmetry in physical observables. This approach allows researchers to treat magnetic flux as a continuous parameter and avoids the need for large, extended unit cells. The method's accuracy is validated against established MPS simulations for the bosonic Harper-Hofstadter-Hubbard model.
main_problem: Simulating interacting many-body systems in a uniform magnetic field is difficult because the choice of gauge breaks standard translation symmetry, necessitating large, computationally expensive magnetic unit cells.
main_result: The authors developed a gauge-covariant PEPS ansatz that uses virtual flux tensors to maintain translation invariance of physical observables, allowing for efficient simulations using a single unit cell regardless of the magnetic flux value.
method: A new Projected Entangled Pair State (PEPS) ansatz is constructed by imprinting virtual U(1) flux tensors onto the tensor bonds, optimized via a single-site boundary MPS algorithm and gradient-based variational optimization.
model_or_system: The study focuses on the bosonic Harper-Hofstadter-Hubbard model on a two-dimensional square lattice, featuring hopping strength t, on-site repulsion U, and a uniform magnetic flux per plaquette phi.
key_observables: Ground state energy per site and physical expectation values.
important_parameters: Hopping strength (t), interaction strength (U), magnetic flux per plaquette (phi), and particle filling (rho).
main_assumptions: The ansatz construction is motivated by the strong interaction (infinite-U) limit and first-order perturbation theory; the method is demonstrated for bosons but is applicable to fermions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the Peierls phase pattern in the Landau gauge; Figure 2 compares the ground state energy per site calculated with the proposed PEPS method against MPS results on cylinders.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of gauge-dependent symmetry breaking, proposes a gauge-covariant PEPS ansatz based on perturbative expansion, demonstrates the method's gauge independence and symmetry preservation, provides numerical benchmarks against MPS, and includes an appendix proving gauge covariance via flux tensor reconfiguration.
why_interesting: This provides a powerful new tool for simulating topological phases and fractional Chern insulators in lattice models without the computational bottleneck of large magnetic unit cells.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Graphical representation of the Harper-Hofstadter- Hubbard Hamiltonian [Eq. (7)] with hopping strength t, on-site repulsion U and magnetic flux per plaquette ϕ = 2πa2B/Φ0. We have chosen the Landau gauge A = −Bx ey, leading to the indicated pattern of Peierls phases ϕij in the hopping terms −t(eiϕijb† ibj + h.c.). The arrows indicate the orientation of the line integral defining ϕij, while the labels ϕ, 2ϕ, 3ϕ, . . . specify the magnitude of the phase accumulated along that direction.

FIG. 2. Ground state energy per site of the bosonic Harper- Hofstadter-Hubbard model [Eq.(7)] at U/t = 20 and unit fill- ing as a function of the flux per plaquette ϕ, computed with our variational PEPS ansatz on the infinite plane, compared to MPS simulation on an infinite cylinder of width Ly = 6 and Ly = 7.
